content.full_text:
· State of California 
¢ 
Memorandum 
To 
John K. 
Nan 
Gina 
Jay 
Mike 
Ken F. 
Natalie 
Mickey 
Dan 
Bob S. 
Barbara 
Fred 
Rich M. 
Date 
Nov. 10, 1976 
From 
F AIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ted Prim 
Subject: 
Question from Tony Alperin concerning 
application of Section 81005. 
1. 
XYZ Committee supports both an assemblyman and the mayor 
of Bakersfield in an election. 
Section 8l005(b) requires 
committees supporting assemblymen to file an "original" 
campaign statement and one copy with the Secretary of 
State. 
Section 8l005(d) requires committees supporting 
city candidates to file an "original" campaign statement 
and one copy with the City Clerk. 
Both sections also 
require "copies" of campaign statements to be filed with 
various other persons. 
Pursuant to Section 91013, origi-
nals and copies are treated differently with respect to 
the imposition of late filing penalties. 
The question 
before the Opinion Request Meeting of October 5 was whether 
only one of the committee's statements could be an original 
or whether several of the statements filed by the committee 
could be originals pursuant to the various subsections of 
Section 81005. 
The staff concluded that the subsections of Section 81005 
were independent, and thus, several of the statements filed 
by the committee could be deemed originals. 
Therefore, with 
respect to the facts stated above, both the statement filed 
with the Secretary of State and the statement filed with the 
Bakersfield City Clerk would be originals for the purpose of 
assessing late filing penalties pursuant to Section 91013. 
2. 
Section 8l005(b) requires that each committee supporting 
a candidate for elective state office file, in addition 
to the "original" and one copy of the campaign statement 
filed with the Secretary of State, two copies of the 
statement with the clerk of the largest county in which 
the committee supported a candidate for elective state 
office during the period. 
Section 8l005(e) requires that 
an "original" and one copy of the campaign statement be 
filed with the clerk of any county in which the committee 


Memorandum 
Page 2 
Nov. 10, 1976 
supports a candidate for county supervisor. 
If the XYZ Com-
mittee supports an elective state candidate in Los Angeles 
and a Los Angeles County supervisor, Subsections 8l005(b) and 
8l005(e) would both apply. 
Under the literal language of the 
subsections, the committee would be required to file two copies 
of the campaign statement with the Registrar-Recorder because 
the committee supports an elective state candidate and an origi-
nal plus a copy with the Registrar-Recorder because it supports 
a candidate for county supervisor. 
Because the original and 
the copies are identical statements, we obviously would only 
require that two documents, not four, be filed with the clerk. 
The question before the staff at the October 5 Opinion Request 
Meeting was whether, for the purpose of imposing fines pursu-
ant to Section 91013, one of the documents filed should be 
treated as an original. 
The staff concluded that it should be 
treated as an original. 
TP:jo

Nov. 10, 1976

sections.conclusion: The staff concluded that the subsections of Section 81005 were independent, and thus, several of the statements filed by the committee could be deemed originals. Therefore, both the statement filed with the Secretary of State and the statement filed with the Bakersfield City Clerk would be originals for the purpose of assessing late filing penalties pursuant to Section 91013. Additionally, when requirements overlap (state candidate and county supervisor in same county), the staff concluded that one of the documents filed should be treated as an original for purposes of imposing fines.
sections.question_synthetic: The FPPC addressed whether committees supporting multiple candidates at different government levels must file single originals or multiple originals, and how late filing penalties apply when filing requirements under different subsections of Section 81005 overlap.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that subsections of Section 81005 are independent, allowing multiple statements to be treated as originals for purposes of late filing penalties. When filing requirements overlap due to supporting candidates at different levels, the identical statements filed need only be treated as resulting in one original document for penalty assessment purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Internal memorandum documenting staff conclusions from Opinion Request Meeting of October 5, 1976. Contains numbered questions and corresponding conclusions but formatted as internal staff analysis rather than formal advice letter response. OCR quality appears good with minor formatting artifacts.

QUESTION: from Tony Alperin concerning 
application of Section 81005. 
1. 
XYZ Committee supports both an assemblyman and the mayor 
of Bakersfield in an election. 
Section 8l005(b) requires 
committees supporting assemblymen to file an "original" 
campaign statement and one copy with the Secretary of 
State. 
Section 8l005(d) requires committees supporting 
city candidates to file an "original" campaign statement 
and one copy with the City Clerk. 
Both sections also 
require "copies" of campaign statements to be filed with 
various other persons. 
Pursuant to Section 91013, origi-
nals and copies are treated differently with respect to 
the imposition of late filing penalties. 
The question 
before the Opinion Request Meeting of October 5 was whether 
only one of the committee's statements could be an original 
or whether several of the statements filed by the committee 
could be originals pursuant to the various subsections of 
Section 81005. 
The staff concluded that the subsections of Section 81005 
were independent, and thus, several of the statements filed 
by the committee could be deemed originals. 
Therefore, with 
respect to the facts stated above, both the statement filed 
with the Secretary of State and the statement filed with the 
Bakersfield City Clerk would be originals for the purpose of 
assessing late filing penalties pursuant to Section 91013. 
2. 
Section 8l005(b) requires that each committee supporting 
a candidate for elective state office file, in addition 
to the "original" and one copy of the campaign statement 
filed with the Secretary of State, two copies of the 
statement with the clerk of the largest county in which 
the committee supported a candidate for elective state 
office during the period. 
Section 8l005(e) requires that 
an "original" and one copy of the campaign statement be 
filed with the clerk of any county in which the committee 


Memorandum  
Nov. 10, 1976 
supports a candidate for county supervisor. 
If the XYZ Com-
mittee supports an elective state candidate in Los Angeles 
and a Los Angeles County supervisor, Subsections 8l005(b) and 
8l005(e) would both apply. 
Under the literal language of the 
subsections, the committee would be required to file two copies 
of the campaign statement with the Registrar-Recorder because 
the committee supports an elective state candidate and an origi-
nal plus a copy with the Registrar-Recorder because it supports 
a candidate for county supervisor. 
Because the original and 
the copies are identical statements, we obviously would only 
require that two documents, not four, be filed with the clerk. 
The question before the staff at the October 5 Opinion Request 
Meeting was whether, for the purpose of imposing fines pursu-
ant to Section 91013, one of the documents filed should be 
treated as an original. 
The staff concluded that it should be 
treated as an original. 
TP:jo

CONCLUSION: The staff concluded that the subsections of Section 81005 were independent, and thus, several of the statements filed by the committee could be deemed originals. Therefore, both the statement filed with the Secretary of State and the statement filed with the Bakersfield City Clerk would be originals for the purpose of assessing late filing penalties pursuant to Section 91013. Additionally, when requirements overlap (state candidate and county supervisor in same county), the staff concluded that one of the documents filed should be treated as an original for purposes of imposing fines.

Pursuant to Section 91013, origi- nals and copies are treated differently with respect to the imposition of late filing penalties. The staff concluded that the subsections of Section 81005 were independent, and thus, several of the statements filed by the committee could be deemed originals. Section 8l005(e) requires that an "original" and one copy of the campaign statement be filed with the clerk of any county in which the committee Memorandum Page 2 Nov. 10, 1976 supports a candidate for county supervisor. Under the literal language of the subsections, the committee would be required to file two copies of the campaign statement with the Registrar-Recorder because the committee supports an elective state candidate and an origi- nal plus a copy with the Registrar-Recorder because it supports a candidate for county supervisor. The staff concluded that it should be treated as an original. TP:jo
embedding.summary: This memorandum addresses FPPC staff conclusions on how Section 81005 campaign filing requirements apply when committees support candidates at multiple government levels, specifically whether multiple original statements can be filed and how late filing penalties are assessed under overlapping filing requirements.
